title: A completed queue item handoff does not create new execution authority

observed: M-001 is marked completed in the canonical queue.
M-001 contains a handoff describing possible next continuity work.
CURRENT_STATE requires teams to update only owned records.
inferred: A handoff records continuity and recommendation, but is not itself an active queue assignment.
Executing handoff work without creating a new owned record would blur historical completion with current authorization.
proposed: Future continuity work derived from a completed item's handoff should receive a new queue item or an additive queue-update record before being represented as owned active work.
Completion, handoff, authorization, and execution evidence should remain separate linked states.
superseded: The implicit rule that a completed item's handoff can be treated as an active queue item without a new ownership record.
unresolved: The repository does not yet define a canonical schema for promoting a handoff into a newly authorized queue item.
No active continuity_mining queue item exists at this observation point.

design_rule.name: handoff_authority_boundary
design_rule.transition_requirement: handoff_proposed may become authorized only through a new explicit owner-bearing queue or queue-update record
design_rule.fail_closed_behavior: when authorization is absent, preserve the handoff as proposed and do not claim active ownership or completion

falsification_or_review_route: Falsify this boundary only if a canonical repository rule explicitly grants active execution authority through a completed item's handoff field alone. Otherwise verify by reading ACTIVE_QUEUE.json and CURRENT_STATE.json at the recorded blobs.
